question_text: A tug-of-war rope has forces acting on it from both teams. Team A pulls with a force of 800 N to the left, and Team B pulls with a force of 800 N to the right. Describe what happens to the rope and explain your answer using the concept of resultant force.
correct_answer: The rope remains stationary (does not move). The resultant force is zero because the two forces are equal in size but opposite in direction, so they balance each other out.
explanation: When two equal forces act in opposite directions, they cancel each other out completely, giving a resultant force of zero. According to Newton's first law, the rope will remain at rest because there is no unbalanced force to cause it to accelerate in any direction.
